name: com.cidonix.unibridge
description: Local MCP tools that let AI coding agents inspect, edit, capture, and validate Unity Editor projects through a per-project bridge and relay.

_upm.changelog:
### Improved

- WaitForReadyAfterReload now returns buildSystemHealth and assemblyFreshness once at the top level, while nested compilationDiagnostics is limited to retained CompilationPipeline/editor event diagnostics. This keeps reload checkpoint responses easier for AI agents to scan without removing the critical build-system evidence added in 0.2.30.
- Reload-safe relay recovery envelopes now strip nested structuredContent mirrors from embedded Unity tool results, reducing duplicate payloads after refresh, compile, and Play Mode reload boundaries.

### Previous Changes

- BuildProgram/Bee failures such as internal build system errors, FileLoadException, Code Integrity blocks, and Application Control policy blocks are classified as critical console diagnostics instead of ordinary logs.
- GetCompilationDiagnostics includes buildSystemHealth and Assembly-CSharp freshness evidence so agents can detect stale runtime assemblies even when CompilationPipeline diagnostics are empty.
- ReadConsole Search matches stack traces as well as visible message lines, so blocked BuildProgram dependencies such as NiceIO.dll can be found directly.
